articles.full_text:
Early summer hits Gujarat as temperature crosses 41°C:17 districts under heatwave alert, scorching heat expected to continue
- Copy link
The mercury level has risen sky-high in Gujarat affecting public life, with major cities including Rajkot, Ahmedabad, Deesa and Kandla crossing temperatures above 41 degrees.
The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert in 17 districts of the state, while the severe heat impact is predicted to continue for the next 3 days.
Ahmedabad municipal corporation issues precautionary advisory
In Ahmedabad, where the temperature was recorded 7 degrees above normal, the Municipal Corporation has also issued precautionary advisories. However, coastal areas will experience lower temperatures but will feel uncomfortable due to humidity. After three days of intense heat, there is a possibility of slight relief for people as temperatures may drop by 2 to 3 degrees.
Rajkot and Kandla likely to record highest temperature today
According to IMD and Weather Department reports, temperatures in most cities may be recorded 3 degrees Celsius to 7 degrees Celsius higher than normal. Rajkot and Kandla may record the highest temperature today, where the mercury is expected to cross 41.5 degrees Celsius. Coastal areas like Dwarka, Okha and Veraval will have relatively lower temperatures, but humidity may cause sultry conditions.
Heat wave alert today in 17 districts
Ahmedabad is also forecasted to experience scorching heat today, which is about 7 degrees higher than normal. Heat wave alerts have been issued for Banaskantha, Patan, Mehsana, Gandhinagar, Ahmedabad, Surendranagar, Morbi, Rajkot, Junagadh, Porbandar, Amreli, Bhavnagar, Botad, Vadodara, Bharuch, Surat and Kutch.
Temperature could reach up to 45 to 46 degrees this year
According to another weather expert A.T. Desai, “The maximum temperature in March this year could reach between 41 to 43 degrees Celsius. During the season, the maximum temperature could reach up to 45 to 46 degrees Celsius”.
Heat wave effect to continue in Gujarat for next 3 days
According to the forecast, the heat wave effect will continue in Gujarat for the next 3 days. Temperature in cities including Ahmedabad, Rajkot, and Surendranagar could reach up to 41-42 degrees. There is a possibility of some relief from the heat after that, with temperature dropping by 2-3 degrees.
In Ahmedabad, the maximum temperature has already crossed 41 degrees Celsius. There will be no major change in temperature for the next three days, but some relief from heat may be possible after that. The sky will remain clear in the city and there is a possibility of intense sunlight.
The 1200-bed hospital at Civil will be made centrally air-conditioned due to the heat
To ensure that patients and their relatives visiting the Civil Hospital do not face difficulties during the summer heat, a plan has been initiated to make the 1200-bed hospital centrally air-conditioned. Considering the rising temperatures, 80 air coolers will be installed in various wards of the 1200-bed hospital and the old Civil Hospital.
Civil Hospital Superintendent Dr Rakesh Joshi said, “There is a plan to make the 1200-bed hospital centrally air-conditioned and approval for the project has already been received from the government. Work on the project will begin soon. Facilities for air conditioning already exist in the Trauma Centre and in blocks such as O and D-3 and D-5. Gradually, air-conditioning facilities will be extended to all wards of the entire 1200-bed hospital”.
Mobile water service to start in the campus
More than 10,000 patients and their relatives visit different hospitals in the Civil campus every day. To ensure they have access to drinking water during the heat, a mobile water distribution service using e-rickshaws will be started from 9 AM to 6 PM.

articles.title: Intense Heat Melts Roads In Gujarat's Navsari, People's Shoes Getting Stuck In Asphalt | PICS - News18
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Gujarat
articles.full_text:
Intense Heat Melts Roads In Gujarat's Navsari, People's Shoes Getting Stuck In Asphalt | PICS
Last Updated:
Shocking scenes in Navsari as temperatures hit 39.9°C. The scorching heat melted road asphalt, leaving pedestrians struggling as their slippers stuck to the road
1/10
Even before the summer season has fully set in, scorching temperatures are already making their presence felt across parts of South Gujarat. In Navsari district, the temperature climbed to 39.9°C, leading to unusual and concerning scenes on city roads as residents struggled to cope with the intense heat.
2/10
The extreme heat created alarming conditions along the main road connecting the Navsari Tower area to Surat. The asphalt on the road reportedly softened and began to melt due to the high temperature, making the surface resemble muddy ground.
ADVERTISEMENT
3/10
Pedestrians found it difficult to cross the road as their slippers began sticking to the softened asphalt while walking. Several people reportedly struggled to move across the road, highlighting the severity of the heat and the impact it was having on daily life in the city.
4/10
During the afternoon hours, many streets in Navsari appear nearly deserted as people prefer to remain indoors to escape the heat. Those who step outside are often seen covering their faces with cloths or wearing caps and hats to protect themselves from the strong sunlight.
ADVERTISEMENT
5/10
The heatwave-like conditions are not limited to Navsari alone. Several parts of Gujarat are witnessing a sharp rise in temperatures. Cities such as Surat, Bhavnagar, Veraval, Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar and Porbandar have recorded temperatures exceeding 36°C.
6/10
Humidity levels are making conditions even more uncomfortable in coastal regions, while interior areas of the state are experiencing even higher temperatures ranging between 38°C and 39°C. Both Gandhinagar and Ahmedabad have already crossed the 38°C mark during the first week of March.
ADVERTISEMENT
7/10
According to the Weather Report Bulletin issued by the India Meteorological Department’s Ahmedabad Meteorological Centre, winds across Gujarat are currently blowing from the north-west to the north. The department has indicated that dry weather conditions are likely to continue across the state until March 13. Weather experts warn that if the current trend continues, temperatures could rise even further in the coming weeks and may potentially cross 45°C during the peak summer months.
8/10
Experts have advised residents to take extra precautions while stepping outdoors during this intense heat. People are encouraged to avoid going out during the afternoon hours when the sun is at its strongest. If stepping outside becomes unavoidable, individuals should carry umbrellas, wear hats or cover their heads with cloth to protect themselves from direct sunlight.
ADVERTISEMENT
9/10

articles.title: Heat scorches Gujarat; mercury crosses 41°C in Ahmedabad, Rajkot, other cities - DeshGujarat
articles.search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day") Gujarat
articles.full_text:
Heat scorches Gujarat; mercury crosses 41°C in Ahmedabad, Rajkot, other cities
March 10, 2026
Ahmedabad: Temperatures climbed sharply across Gujarat on Monday, with Ahmedabad recording a maximum of 41.2°C, about 7°C above normal, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD). The city’s minimum temperature was 21.8°C, nearly 3.9°C above normal.
Meanwhile, Rajkot and Surendranagar recorded the highest temperatures in the state at 41.6°C each. They were followed by Kandla Port (41.4°C), Ahmedabad (41.2°C), Deesa (41.1°C) and Keshod (40.8°C).
Gandhinagar and Surat both registered 40.6°C, while Vadodara and Bhuj recorded 40.4°C. Amreli reported 40.5°C.
Coastal areas remained relatively cooler due to the sea breeze effect. Dwarka recorded 28.4°C, Okha 29.2°C, and Veraval 31.2°C, while Porbandar reported 37.8°C and Daman 37°C.
During the last 24 hours, hot and humid weather prevailed over Gujarat, Saurashtra-Kutch, and the Union Territories of Diu, Daman, Dadra and Nagar Haveli. Maximum temperatures remained above to markedly above normal in several places, while minimum temperatures also increased in parts of Gujarat and Saurashtra-Kutch.
Additionally, the IMD has predicted clear skies in Ahmedabad on Tuesday, with the maximum temperature likely to remain around 41°C and the minimum close to 22°C. DeshGujarat
Today’s Weather Report (Reported on 0830 IST)
